name: Ashcombe Maze & Lavender Gardens
signature: Lavender fields, Australia's oldest hedge maze, rose garden, and a small café  -  the quiet, slightly wholesome Peninsula detour
editorNote:
Ashcombe Maze is the Peninsula's most unexpectedly charming detour. The gardens hold Australia's oldest symmetrical hedge maze, a circular rose garden, a lavender labyrinth, and a small café that sells lavender everything  -  ice cream, scones, shortbread, tisane  -  from a room that smells exactly like the fields outside.

The place is deliberately low-tempo. You walk the maze, you sit in the lavender, you drink tea, and you leave feeling like you have stumbled into a slightly older, gentler version of the Peninsula. It is a good rainy-afternoon reset and an even better spring morning plan when the lavender is up.

Bring the kids. Bring older visitors. Do not try to be cynical about it  -  the charm wins.
